subject: Ecology
title: Wetland Hydrology
description: A quick, practical guide to Wetland Hydrology.

hook.text: Water controls everything in a wetland—how deep, how long, and when it arrives.

buildup.text: The hydroperiod—the pattern of flooding and drying—determines which species can survive.

discovery.text: Permanent wetlands support fish. Seasonal ones favor amphibians that need predator-free pools.

twist.text: A small change in water level—just centimeters—can shift a wetland from marsh to open lake.

climax.text: Altering hydrology through dams, drainage, or pumping destroys wetlands faster than any other threat.

punchline.text: Control the water and you control the wetland.

quiz.question: What is a hydroperiod?
options: The pattern of flooding and drying in a wetland
The total rainfall in a year
The depth of the water table
